title: Google Student Internships
description: Paid student roles across software engineering, product, research, and business functions.
eligibility: Current students who meet the requirements of an individual posted role.

title: Apple Student Internships
description: Internships across hardware, software, machine learning, operations, and corporate teams.
eligibility: Students enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program; role requirements vary.

title: NVIDIA University Internships
description: Technical and business internships in accelerated computing, AI, graphics, and related fields.
eligibility: Current undergraduate and graduate students meeting role-specific qualifications.

title: IBM Internships
description: Student roles in software, AI, research, consulting, design, and business.
eligibility: Current students who meet the qualifications listed for an open internship.

title: Bloomberg Internships
description: Internships in engineering, data, product, finance, and customer-facing teams.
eligibility: Students meeting the requirements of a listed internship opening.

title: Palantir Student Internships
description: Student opportunities in software engineering, deployment strategy, and related technical roles.
eligibility: Current students meeting role-specific technical and location requirements.

title: Jane Street Internships
description: Internships in quantitative trading, research, software engineering, and strategy.
eligibility: Students meeting the requirements of the specific internship track.

title: Citadel Internships
description: Internships in investing, quantitative research, engineering, and operations.
eligibility: Students who meet the academic and technical requirements of a posted role.

title: Citadel Securities Internships
description: Internships in quantitative research, trading, and engineering for global markets.
eligibility: Students meeting requirements for a listed internship discipline.

title: Two Sigma Internships
description: Quantitative research, software engineering, modeling, and business internships.
eligibility: Current students meeting the requirements of an open internship.

title: Hudson River Trading Internships
description: Student roles in algorithm development, software engineering, and quantitative research.
eligibility: Students meeting the requirements of an open HRT student role.

title: SIG Campus Internships
description: Internships in trading, quantitative research, technology, and business operations.
eligibility: Students who meet the qualifications of a listed campus role.

title: IMC Student Internships
description: Internships in trading, software engineering, and quantitative research.
eligibility: Students meeting the requirements of a listed student or graduate role.

title: Optiver Student Internships
description: Trading, research, and technology internships within a global market maker.
eligibility: Students meeting role-specific academic, technical, and location requirements.

title: DRW Internships
description: Internships across trading, quantitative research, software, and infrastructure.
eligibility: Students meeting qualifications for an internship listing.

title: Summer Analyst Program
description: Summer analyst placements across engineering, finance, operations, and business divisions.
eligibility: Undergraduate students graduating within the program’s specified window.

title: Software Engineer Program Internship
description: Software engineering internship working on technology used across financial services.
eligibility: Students meeting graduation-date, degree, and technical requirements for a listed location.

title: Morgan Stanley Summer Analyst Programs
description: Summer programs spanning finance, technology, operations, and institutional businesses.
eligibility: Students meeting the graduation and location requirements of a posted program.

title: BlackRock Summer Internship Program
description: Summer internships across investments, analytics, technology, and corporate functions.
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students meeting the requirements of a listed role and region.

title: NASA OSTEM Internships
description: Internships supporting NASA missions in science, engineering, technology, and business.
eligibility: Eligibility varies by opportunity and may include citizenship, enrollment, GPA, and age requirements.

title: Amazon Student Internships
description: Internships in software, operations, product, science, design, and business.
eligibility: Current students who meet the requirements of a posted university role.

title: Adobe Internships
description: Student roles in software, AI, design, product, research, and business.
eligibility: Current students meeting the requirements of a listed internship.

title: SpaceX Internships
description: Engineering, software, manufacturing, and business internships supporting spaceflight programs.
eligibility: Currently enrolled students meeting academic, technical, and work-authorization requirements.

title: Tesla Internships
description: Internships in engineering, software, manufacturing, energy, design, and business.
eligibility: Students meeting role-specific enrollment, technical, and location requirements.

title: Datadog Internships
description: Early-career roles in software engineering, product, security, and related teams.
eligibility: Students meeting requirements for an open internship or early-career role.

title: Student Training in Engineering Program (STEP)
description: Development-focused software engineering internship for students early in their university studies.
eligibility: Students meeting the current location, enrollment, and early-college requirements on Google’s program page.

title: Explore Microsoft
description: Early-college internship experience combining software engineering disciplines and product development.
eligibility: Students meeting Microsoft’s current early-college, degree, and location requirements.

title: First-Year Trading and Technology Program
description: Educational program introducing first-year students to trading, software, and problem solving.
eligibility: First-year students meeting the current program’s location and participation requirements.

title: Summer in Engineering (SEE)
description: Early-college program focused on software engineering concepts and collaborative problem solving.
eligibility: Students meeting the current class-year and program eligibility requirements.

title: Women in STEM Education (WiSE)
description: Program introducing eligible students to quantitative finance, technology, and Jane Street.
eligibility: Students meeting the program’s current class-year and participation criteria.

title: Discover Citadel
description: Early-identification program introducing students to investing, quantitative research, and technology careers.
eligibility: Students meeting the current academic-year and program requirements.

title: UberSTAR
description: Software engineering internship program for students early in their undergraduate studies.
eligibility: Students meeting current class-year, degree, and location requirements for a posted UberSTAR role.

title: Duolingo Thrive Internship Program
description: Early-career internship pathway designed to broaden access to software and product careers.
eligibility: Students meeting the current Thrive role’s class-year and participation requirements.

title: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates Sites
description: Directory of NSF-funded summer research sites across science and engineering disciplines.
eligibility: Undergraduates; citizenship or permanent-residency restrictions commonly apply to NSF-funded positions.

title: Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internships (SULI)
description: Research appointments at Department of Energy national laboratories.
eligibility: Undergraduate students or recent associate/bachelor’s graduates meeting citizenship, GPA, and enrollment rules.

title: Community College Internships (CCI)
description: Research and technical projects for community-college students at DOE laboratories.
eligibility: Community-college students meeting citizenship, enrollment, coursework, and GPA requirements.

title: NIH Summer Internship Program
description: Biomedical research placements in NIH intramural laboratories and research groups.
eligibility: Students meeting NIH age, enrollment, citizenship or residency, and location requirements.

title: NIST Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship
organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Summer research with NIST scientists and engineers in measurement science and technology.
eligibility: Undergraduate students meeting the current program’s citizenship, enrollment, and academic requirements.

title: Amgen Scholars Program
description: Summer biomedical and biotechnology research at participating host institutions.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting the regional program and host institution requirements.

title: MIT Summer Research Program
description: Faculty-mentored summer research for eligible visiting undergraduates interested in graduate study.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting MIT’s current academic, background, and graduate-study interest requirements.

title: Stanford Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship
description: Summer research and graduate-school preparation for visiting undergraduates in engineering.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting the program’s current academic and participation requirements.

title: Caltech Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowships
description: Student-designed summer research projects conducted with a Caltech or JPL mentor.
eligibility: Undergraduates who secure an eligible mentor and meet current proposal requirements.

title: Research Experiences for Undergraduates in Software Engineering
description: Summer research in software engineering with Carnegie Mellon faculty and research groups.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting current program, citizenship, and academic requirements.

title: SUPERB Information Technology for Sustainability
description: Summer undergraduate research in electrical engineering and computer science.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting current NSF and host-program eligibility requirements.

title: DIMACS REU
description: Summer research projects in discrete mathematics and theoretical computer science.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting current program and NSF eligibility requirements.

title: HackMIT
description: Annual student hackathon centered on building technical projects in teams.
eligibility: Current students meeting the event’s application, age, and participation rules.

title: PennApps
description: Student-run collegiate hackathon for software, hardware, and interdisciplinary projects.
eligibility: Students meeting the event’s current application and participation requirements.

title: TreeHacks
description: Stanford’s annual student hackathon for technical and interdisciplinary projects.
eligibility: Students meeting the current event’s application and participation requirements.

title: Cal Hacks
description: Large collegiate hackathon supporting student-built technology projects.
eligibility: Students meeting the current event’s application, age, and conduct rules.

title: Hack the North
description: International student hackathon hosted by University of Waterloo students.
eligibility: Students meeting the current event’s application, travel, and participation requirements.

title: HackGT
description: Georgia Tech student hackathon for software, hardware, and creative technology projects.
eligibility: Students meeting the event’s application and participation requirements.

title: MHacks
description: Student-led hackathon for building and presenting technology projects.
eligibility: Students meeting current event eligibility and registration requirements.

title: LA Hacks
description: Student hackathon bringing together builders and designers in Los Angeles.
eligibility: Students meeting current application and participation requirements.

title: TAMUhack
description: Student-run hackathon for technical projects, learning, and collaboration.
eligibility: Students meeting current registration and event participation requirements.

title: VandyHacks
description: Collegiate hackathon for students to create technology projects and learn new skills.
eligibility: Students meeting current event application and participation requirements.

title: HackHarvard
description: Student hackathon for technical and interdisciplinary projects.
eligibility: Students meeting current application and event requirements.

title: BoilerMake
description: Purdue student hackathon for developing technical projects over a weekend.
eligibility: Students meeting current event application and participation requirements.

title: International Collegiate Programming Contest
description: Team-based competitive programming contest progressing through regional and world stages.
eligibility: Students whose institution and team meet current ICPC regional eligibility rules.

title: William Lowell Putnam Mathematical Competition
description: Annual mathematics competition for undergraduate students in the United States and Canada.
eligibility: Eligible undergraduate students registered through a participating institution.

title: ASA DataFest
description: Team data-analysis competition hosted at participating institutions.
eligibility: Undergraduates participating through an official host site; local rules apply.

title: Microsoft Imagine Cup
description: Global student technology competition for teams building solutions with Microsoft technologies.
eligibility: Students meeting current age, enrollment, team, and country requirements.

title: Hult Prize
description: Global student competition for entrepreneurial ventures addressing social challenges.
eligibility: Student teams meeting the current competition and campus pathway rules.

title: NASA International Space Apps Challenge
description: Global challenge using NASA data to address space and Earth science problems.
eligibility: Open participation subject to local event registration and current challenge rules.

title: MLH Fellowship
description: Project-based technical fellowship with open-source, production engineering, or related tracks.
eligibility: Applicants meeting the current age, availability, location, and technical requirements.

title: Kleiner Perkins Fellows
description: Fellowship connecting students with engineering, design, and product roles at portfolio companies.
eligibility: Students meeting current track, graduation, work-authorization, and location requirements.

title: 8VC Fellowship
description: Technology fellowship pairing students with roles and programming across 8VC portfolio companies.
eligibility: Students meeting current fellowship, role, and location requirements.

title: Neo Scholars
description: Community and mentorship program for technically ambitious university students.
eligibility: Students meeting the current application, age, and program participation requirements.

title: Brooke Owens Fellowship
description: Paid aerospace internship and mentorship fellowship for eligible undergraduate women and gender minorities.
eligibility: Undergraduates meeting the fellowship’s current identity, enrollment, and work requirements.

title: Grace Hopper Celebration Student Opportunities
description: Student participation, scholarship, and career opportunities connected to the Grace Hopper Celebration.
eligibility: Eligibility varies by registration, scholarship, volunteer, and current conference programs.

title: CMD-IT/ACM Tapia Conference
description: Computing conference focused on broadening participation, research, careers, and student development.
eligibility: Students may apply for current scholarships, poster programs, or registration opportunities when offered.

title: SACNAS National Diversity in STEM Conference
description: STEM conference with research presentations, professional development, mentoring, and recruiting.
eligibility: Students meeting current registration, presentation, travel-scholarship, or volunteer requirements.

title: NSBE Annual Convention
description: Engineering convention with career fairs, technical sessions, competitions, and student programming.
eligibility: Registration and program eligibility vary by membership and current convention rules.

title: Millennium Fellowship
organization: Millennium Campus Network and United Nations Academic Impact
description: Semester-long leadership development program for campus-based social impact projects.
eligibility: Undergraduate students at participating campuses who meet current cohort requirements.

title: GitHub Campus Experts
description: Training and support for students building technical communities on campus.
eligibility: Students meeting GitHub Education verification and current program requirements.

title: Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors
description: Student community program centered on technical learning, peer leadership, and campus events.
eligibility: Students meeting current age, enrollment, conduct, and participation requirements.
